title: rubus luchunensis var. coriaceus
intro: Rubus luchunensis var. coriaceus, also known as the Chinese raspberry, is a type of plant that belongs to the Rosaceae family, characterized by its thorny stems and edible fruits. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus luchunensis var. coriaceus include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting cardiovascular health. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant can be consumed as a tea, made from dried leaves and fruits, or used as a decoction to make a medicinal soup.
